marketState: neutral / mixed — bearish short-term technicals vs a constructive longer-term demand & positioning setup
summary: Price ($2.84 front) sits essentially FAIR against the model at every horizon (spreads of only -$0.08 to -$0.18), while fair value itself drifts up from $2.92 now to $3.00 by +21D, arguing there's no valuation edge in either direction today. Technicals are uniformly bearish from 5m through 1w with 50<200 EMA crosses on every timeframe, but daily/weekly RSI (34-35) is getting stretched and managed money is already net short at the 8th percentile of its 5-year range — a crowded positioning setup that raises squeeze risk on any fresh short. The 8-16 day weather outlook is running +21.7% above 5-year normal cooling demand (rangePos 0.89), a genuinely hot signal that argues for firmer gas burn into peak season, even though the 1-7D window is only marginally above normal. Storage is a headwind: a +22 Bcf bearish surprise and a +180 Bcf surplus vs the 5-year average keep a lid on upside. Seasonality itself is muted and unreliable this week (10-90% spread of 59pp), so it gets little weight versus the other inputs.

longTerm.rationale: Managed-money shorts are at an 8th-percentile 5y extreme (contrarian bullish/squeeze setup) and 8-16D cooling demand is running +21.7% above normal near the top of its range, while fair value itself rises to $3.00 by +21D with price still trading fractionally under fair at every horizon.
longTerm.invalidation: A larger-than-forecast storage build/surprise turning consistently bearish, or 8-16D demand rolling back toward/below the 5y normal, would remove the main bullish drivers.

shortTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk, trim size given squeeze risk
shortTerm.rationale: All six technical timeframes (5m-1w) show bearish trend with 50-under-200 EMA crosses, price is only FAIR vs the model with no near-term valuation support, and the futures curve shows the front slightly above next (mild backwardation), consistent with soft near-term momentum.
shortTerm.invalidation: A break back above ~$2.98-3.00 with MACD/RSI turning up, or a sharp short-covering move given how crowded managed-money shorts already are, would invalidate this trade.
